nl_statement: Layer-2 single-step sufficient-decrease inequalities for SHB on an L-smooth function f. (i) shb_step_descent : f(x_{t+1}) ≤ f(x_t) + ⟨f'(x_t), x_{t+1}-x_t⟩ + (l/2)‖x_{t+1}-x_t‖²; (ii) shb_step_descent_expanded : same with the recurrence step β•(x_t - x_{t-1}) - η•f'(x_t) substituted; (iii) gd_step_descent : the analogous inequality for plain GD; (iv) shb_step_descent_at_beta_zero : at β = 0, the SHB descent inequality coincides with the GD descent inequality applied to the gradient-descent iterates from x₀.
lean_signature:
namespace SHB.Foundations
variable {E : Type*} [NormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E]
theorem shb_step_descent : ∀ x_t x_{t+1} ∈ SHB orbit, f(x_{t+1}) ≤ f(x_t) + ⟨f'(x_t), x_{t+1}-x_t⟩ + (l/2)‖x_{t+1}-x_t‖²
theorem shb_step_descent_expanded : same with step substituted
theorem gd_step_descent : analogous bound for gdIter
theorem shb_step_descent_at_beta_zero : SHB(β=0) descent = GD descent on gdIter iterates
assumptions: f : E → ℝ has gradient f' pointwise
f' is l-Lipschitz with l : NNReal
reuse_reason: this is the core inequality every Layer-3 SHB convergence proof builds on (telescoping, Lyapunov, etc.)
